ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_group" TEXT /* Release group of the movie (e.g., Toy Story 3) */,
  "worldwide_earnings" REAL /* Worldwide box office earnings */,
  "domestic_earnings" REAL /* Domestic box office earnings */,
  "domestic_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of domestic earnings */,
  "foreign_earnings" REAL /* Foreign box office earnings */,
  "foreign_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of foreign earnings */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of release */,
  "start_year" INTEGER /* Start year of production */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director of the movie */,
  "writer" TEXT /* Writer of the movie */,
  "producer" TEXT /* Producer of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the movie in minutes */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* ID of the rating */,
  "mpaa_rating" TEXT /* MPAA rating of the movie */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the movie */,
  "distributor" TEXT /* Distributor of the movie */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Production company of the movie */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline of the movie */,
  "synopsis" TEXT /* Synopsis of the movie */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie poster */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie trailer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_rating_id FOREIGN KEY ("rating_id") REFERENCES ratings ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "rating_name" TEXT /* Name of the rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., studio executive, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date the user joined */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment or review text */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the review was written */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "box_office_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the performance data */,
  "daily_earnings" REAL /* Earnings for the day */,
  "cumulative_earnings" REAL /* Cumulative earnings up to that date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_box_office_performance_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_assignments" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
column_names: Release group of the movie (e.g., Toy Story 3)
Runtime of the movie in minutes
Role of the user (e.g., studio executive, data analyst, admin)
Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive)
ID of the movie being reviewed
ID of the user who wrote the review
Unique identifier for each performance record
Cumulative earnings up to that date
